posts.author: MsquareAutomation (Msquare Automation Solutions)
posts.cookedHtml:
we built an automation that grabs product data from websites and updates our Monday.com boards. It’s been a game-changer for us, and we wanted to share how we did it! Here’s how it works:

  1. Trigger: Add a website URL to our Monday.com board.
  2. Scrape: Fetch the website’s content.
  3. Extract: Find product URLs and prices.
  4. Update: Automatically update the prices on our Monday.com board.

This saves us time and keeps our data accurate.

image1920×888 80.7 KB

By automating these tasks, we can focus on more important work, reduce errors, and keep our product information up-to-date without manual effort. If you’re looking to simplify your processes and make your team more efficient, let’s connect and explore how automation can help your business too!

If you require additional assistance, please don’t hesitate to reach out to us.

posts.author: Usheenayer (Urhan)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s actually amazing how much this kind of automation frees up time.

posts.author: LillyBates (Lilly)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve built something similar, and it’s been a total time-saver for our team too. We used a similar workflow, where we added URLs to a board and pulled product data directly into our systems. One thing that really helped was automating the form submissions with submitting a form with puppeteer. It made scraping dynamic sites so much easier. I remember when we first set it up, there were a few tweaks, but once everything was running smoothly, we could update the data without any hassle.
